fixes.research_basis: PostgreSQL 16/17 manual, CREATE FUNCTION — "Writing SECURITY DEFINER Functions Safely": set search_path to exclude schemas writable by untrusted users, and because the temporary-table schema is searched first by default, write pg_temp as the LAST search_path entry; the safest form is a fixed search_path plus schema-qualified references. PostgreSQL manual §5.9.3 "The Schema Search Path": pg_catalog is always searched (first, if unlisted), which is why pg_catalog.now() resolves under `search_path = pg_catalog, pg_temp` or even `= ''`. CVE-2018-1058 (and the accompanying "A Guide to CVE-2018-1058" from the PostgreSQL project): unqualified references + a caller-controlled search_path + a writable public schema = function/operator shadowing — the exact latent vector these SECURITY INVOKER trigger functions would expose if flipped to SECURITY DEFINER. CIS PostgreSQL Benchmark (v1.x, sections on privileges and function security): apply least privilege, avoid unnecessary SECURITY DEFINER, and constrain function search_path. OWASP SQL Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet: use parameterized/bound queries — the basis for requiring set_config('app.current_business',$1,true) instead of interpolating into SET, with the policies' `::uuid` cast as input-validation defense-in-depth. Empirical confirmation on the live umi_build_v3 / umi_backfill_v3 clusters: both trigger fns prosecdef=f / proconfig=NULL; api has no CREATE on any app schema; RLS fail-closed on unset (0 rows) and empty/garbage (invalid_text_representation); money ledgers INSERT-only during backfill; 5 inherited SECURITY DEFINER functions present only in the snapshot-derived backfill DB, none in the pristine build.
fixes.risks: All five changes are low-risk. (1) The search_path pins are pure defense-in-depth: current exploitability is already low because api has no CREATE anywhere, so nothing changes functionally today; the only forward caveat is that if a future edit to either trigger body references a non-catalog object it must be schema-qualified or it will fail to resolve — a desirable forcing function, not a regression. Neither pin affects tenant RLS (triggers are not policies) and neither affects the backfill (touch fires on backfill UPDATEs where pg_catalog.now() still resolves as superuser; the append-only guard never fires because the ledgers are INSERT-only during load). (2) The 99_verify no-secdef assertion is intentionally strict: it will (correctly) fail if the shipped clean build ever inherits the prod-snapshot's secdef functions, and it is skipped on the backfill DB by the existing runner, so it will not false-positive there. (3) The backfill pre-flight guard can only ABORT earlier and more legibly than the status quo; against today's verified-clean source it is a no-op. (4) The fail-closed gate depends on api being grantable via SET ROLE (true for the nologin api role) and on tenant.customer having RLS enabled (it does). RESIDUAL / OUT OF THIS AREA: the real breach multiplier is the grants layer — `grant select on all tables in schema umi to api` exposes umi.user.password_hash/password_algorithm and full runtime DML (otp/session/password_reset) to the RLS-confined request role, so any app-layer injection or IDOR that these fixes harden the DB against would still read credentials once inside. Narrowing those grants (per-table column grants on umi, sealing runtime auth tables to worker-only) is owned by the grants/RLS area and should be tracked as the dependent fix; it does not change any recommendation here.

fixes.summary: Ten grounded, defense-in-depth config fixes for the build-v3 RLS/credential/transport surface, each verified against the live umi_build_v3 / umi_backfill_v3 DBs. The linchpin is that the whole tenant-isolation story is currently unwired: policies read current_setting('app.current_business') but the backend sets app.tenant_id (empirically → 0 rows as role api; app.current_business → 20 rows), RLS is ENABLED but never FORCED on all 33 tenant tables, the app/worker pools connect as umi_app/postgres (BYPASSRLS) rather than the least-privilege api role that build-v3 grants to, and the VPS→Supabase link has no TLS config at all. Fixes: (1) unify the GUC name on app.current_business and gate name-parity; (2) nullif-guard every policy cast so a blank GUC fails closed instead of hard-erroring; (3) provision a non-superuser, non-BYPASSRLS LOGIN role that is a member of api and repoint DATABASE_URL_APP to it, with a boot assertion; (4) FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY on every tenant base table + assert relforcerowsecurity and a non-owner login role; (5) attach an append-only trigger to both audit_log tables so immutability survives an accidental re-grant; (6) pin TLS verify-full + CA on both pools and assert pg_stat_ssl; (7) declare the exact extension set (add pg_trgm) and put schema extensions on the search_path so unqualified word_similarity() resolves; (8) add a one-page prod transport/auth gate doc tying each managed/self-managed control to a 99_verify assertion; (9) scrub Umi-internal Slack ids out of café-readable audit_log during backfill; (10) replace api's blanket SELECT on all of umi with catalog-only grants + a SECURITY DEFINER credential-lookup function (so an IDOR/injection can no longer bulk-dump password_hash), and drop DELETE on the runtime auth tables. None applied — proposal only. Fixes preserve tenant RLS, keep the global catalogs readable to api, and do not touch the superuser-run backfill path.

changes.what: Unify the RLS session-variable name on app.current_business (the intended contract) across policies and backend, and add a name-parity gate. The backend currently sets app.tenant_id; policies read app.current_business — verified as role api: `set app.tenant_id` → 0 rows on tenant.customer, `set app.current_business` → 20 rows.
changes.where: Backend: apps/umi-api/src/shared/database/pg.service.ts:91-94 (change the set_config key). Policies stay as-is at docs/migration/build-v3/90_rls.sql:41 (+16 lines). New assertion in docs/migration/build-v3/99_verify.sql.
changes.rationale: PostgreSQL RLS policies are evaluated against a runtime configuration parameter read with current_setting(); if the writer and reader disagree on the parameter name the predicate silently compares against NULL and every row is filtered (fail-closed, but a total app outage that invites a panic loosening or a switch to the BYPASSRLS pool). Choosing app.current_business matches the documented request contract ('SET LOCAL app.current_business') and 90_rls.sql, so only one file (the backend) changes and the 17 policy lines stay put. The CI/DB parity gate encodes the invariant 'the string the code sets == the string the policy reads' so this can never drift again. Ref: PostgreSQL 'Row Security Policies' + current_setting/set_config (System Admin Functions).
changes.verification: As the prod login role: `set app.current_business='<known business>'; select count(*) from tenant.customer` returns >0, and `reset app.current_business; set app.tenant_id='<same>'; select count(*) from tenant.customer` returns 0. Static gate: the 99_verify do-block above raises 0 exceptions (0 tenant policies miss app.current_business; 0 reference app.tenant_id). CI grep gate: the literal set_config key in pg.service.ts equals the GUC name found in 90_rls.sql.

changes.what: Guard every policy cast with nullif(...,'') so an unset OR empty-string GUC both resolve to NULL and fail closed to zero rows, instead of aborting the query. Empirically, as role api with app.current_business='' , `select count(*) from tenant.customer` → ERROR invalid input syntax for type uuid: ""; a query-level nullif does NOT help because the RLS policy predicate itself carries the raw cast (I reproduced the error even with a nullif WHERE clause). nullif('','')::uuid IS NULL is confirmed true.
changes.where: docs/migration/build-v3/90_rls.sql — all policy predicates (the tenant.business policy at 41-42, the dynamic business_id loop at 57-60, and each child-table policy at 66-129).
changes.sql_or_config:
-- Every occurrence of:
--   = current_setting('app.current_business', true)::uuid
-- becomes:
--   = nullif(current_setting('app.current_business', true), '')::uuid
-- e.g. the dynamic loop body (90_rls.sql:57-60):
execute format($f$create policy tenant_isolation on tenant.%I
  using      (business_id = nullif(current_setting('app.current_business', true),'')::uuid)
  with check (business_id = nullif(current_setting('app.current_business', true),'')::uuid)$f$, r.table_name);
-- and the same nullif() wrap inside each child-table EXISTS(...) subquery predicate.
changes.rationale: current_setting(name, true) returns NULL only when the parameter was never set in the session; RESET and the backend's own `userId ?? ''` idiom (pg.service.ts:97) both yield '' , and ''::uuid throws, turning the policy expression into a per-request self-inflicted DoS on every RLS table. Wrapping the cast in nullif collapses both 'never set' and 'set to blank' to NULL, and `col = NULL` is never true → zero rows, the correct fail-closed posture (OWASP: fail securely / deny by default). This is the standard Supabase/PostgreSQL RLS idiom for optional session GUCs.
changes.verification: As the prod login role: `set app.current_business=''; select count(*) from tenant.customer` returns 0 with NO error (today it raises). Static gate in 99_verify: `select count(*) from pg_policies where schemaname='tenant' and (coalesce(qual,'')||coalesce(with_check,'')) like '%current_setting(''app.current_business''%' and (coalesce(qual,'')||coalesce(with_check,'')) not like '%nullif%'` = 0.

changes.what: Provision the missing prod LOGIN role as a non-superuser, non-BYPASSRLS member of api, repoint DATABASE_URL_APP to it (build-v3's api/worker/readonly are all NOLOGIN, and vps-setup.md:147 has the app pool on umi_app while the worker pool is `postgres` = BYPASSRLS), and add a boot assertion that the app pool is not superuser/BYPASSRLS. This closes the silent-RLS-bypass path if DATABASE_URL_APP ever lands on a BYPASSRLS role.
changes.where: New prod-only role SQL (not the from-scratch DDL, which correctly keeps api NOLOGIN); DATABASE_URL_APP in the VPS env (apps/umi-api/docs/vps-setup.md:146,156); boot check in apps/umi-api/src/shared/database/pg.service.ts onModuleInit (45-53).
changes.rationale: PostgreSQL RLS is never applied to a superuser or a role with BYPASSRLS. build-v3 grants request privileges only to api (verified: api 215 / worker 264 / readonly 67 grants; umi_app/umi_worker have zero build-v3 grants), so the prod login role must be a plain member of api. Connecting the request path as `postgres` (as the worker line does) would make app.current_business irrelevant and expose every tenant. A boot-time role-attribute assertion turns a silent isolation failure into a loud refusal to start (CIS PostgreSQL Benchmark: restrict superuser/privileged roles; principle of least privilege). Ref: PostgreSQL 'Row Security Policies' (owner/superuser/BYPASSRLS exemption) + CREATE ROLE.
changes.verification: On the app pool connection: `select rolsuper, rolbypassrls from pg_roles where rolname=current_user` returns (f,f) AND `select pg_has_role(current_user,'api','member')` is true. The onModuleInit assertion above must pass (process refuses to boot otherwise). Documented gate: DATABASE_URL_APP username resolves to api_login, not postgres/umi_app/umi_worker.

changes.what: FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY on every tenant base table so isolation still applies if the backend ever connects as the table owner. Verified: all 33 tenant tables have relrowsecurity=t but relforcerowsecurity=f, and on Supabase the tables are owned by `postgres`, which the worker pool already uses.
changes.where: docs/migration/build-v3/90_rls.sql (append after the enable loop, ~line 62) + assertion in 99_verify.sql.
changes.rationale: ALTER TABLE ... FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY makes policies apply to the table owner too; without it, a connection as the owning role (postgres on Supabase) silently ignores every policy while the catalog still shows RLS 'enabled'. Combined with change #3 (non-owner login role) this is belt-and-suspenders: even a misconfigured owner connection stays scoped. Ref: PostgreSQL ALTER TABLE (FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY) + 'Row Security Policies'; Supabase RLS hardening guidance.
changes.verification: 99_verify assertion above raises 0. Ad hoc: `select count(*) from pg_class c join pg_namespace n on n.oid=c.relnamespace where n.nspname='tenant' and c.relkind='r' and not c.relforcerowsecurity` = 0 (currently 33).

changes.what: Attach an append-only trigger to umi.audit_log and tenant.audit_log so immutability survives an accidental future re-GRANT of UPDATE/DELETE. Today immutability rests only on revoked grants (verified: 0 user triggers on either audit_log; the two money ledgers correctly carry append-only triggers).
changes.where: docs/migration/build-v3/20_tenant.sql (tenant.audit_log, after 495) and 10_umi.sql (umi.audit_log, after 251); the trigger function tenant.tg_append_only already exists (20_tenant.sql:12-17). Assertion in 99_verify.sql.
changes.rationale: Triggers fire for every role including superusers and the table owner (unlike RLS, which owners bypass), so a BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE trigger that RAISEs makes the audit trail genuinely append-only even against postgres and even if a grant is later mistakenly restored — the trigger, not the GRANT, becomes the enforcement of record. Keep the function SECURITY INVOKER and side-effect-free, and in prod own it with a dedicated non-superuser role. Ref: PostgreSQL CREATE TRIGGER; append-only/audit-immutability pattern; CIS 'ensure logging/audit records cannot be altered'.
changes.verification: `update umi.audit_log set action=action where true` and `update tenant.audit_log set action=action` both raise 'table is append-only'. The 99_verify block above raises 0 (>=2 UPDATE/DELETE triggers present; no non-owner role holds UPDATE/DELETE).

changes.what: Pin TLS to verify-full with a CA on both Postgres pools (the VPS→Supabase link) and assert an encrypted connection at boot. grep of umi-api/src for ssl|sslmode|rejectUnauthorized|PGSSL returns zero matches; both pools are `new Pool({connectionString})` with no ssl, and node-postgres does not enable TLS when sslmode is absent.
changes.where: apps/umi-api/src/shared/database/pg.service.ts:28-33 (both pools) + src/shared/config/config.schema.ts:29-31 (add PG_CA_CERT_PATH) + apps/umi-api/.env.example:12-13 + Supabase project setting.
changes.rationale: The backend self-hosts on a VPS and crosses the public internet to Supabase, carrying password hashes, session/OTP/reset token_hashes and all tenant data. node-postgres connects in cleartext when sslmode is unset, and even with TLS performs no certificate validation without an explicit CA/rejectUnauthorized, leaving both eavesdropping and host-spoofing/MITM open. verify-full + pinned CA is the only mode that authenticates the server; asserting pg_stat_ssl.ssl at boot proves the negotiated link is encrypted rather than trusting config. Ref: PostgreSQL libpq SSL modes (verify-full); node-postgres SSL docs; OWASP Transport Layer Protection Cheat Sheet; CIS PostgreSQL 'require TLS/SSL'.
changes.verification: On each pool at boot: `select ssl, version, cipher from pg_stat_ssl where pid=pg_backend_pid()` returns ssl=t with a modern cipher. Negative test: pointing sslrootcert at a wrong CA fails the connection (proves verify, not prefer). Config gate: no DATABASE_URL_* uses sslmode=disable/require/prefer; Supabase 'Enforce SSL' = ON (screenshot/API check).

changes.what: Declare the exact extension set the build actually uses and make schema extensions resolvable on the search_path. build_v3 carries only vector; the app calls unqualified word_similarity() (tenants.repository.ts:239 and 7 other repos) which needs pg_trgm. Verified: pg_trgm is absent from build_v3, and even on umi_backfill_v3 (where it exists in schema extensions) unqualified word_similarity() fails while extensions.word_similarity('a','b') works — because api/worker/readonly have no search_path override (rolconfig NULL).
changes.where: docs/migration/build-v3/00_foundation.sql:22-25 (extensions) + role/database search_path + assertion in 99_verify.sql.
changes.rationale: A from-scratch Supabase build does not auto-create prod extensions, so a shipped feature (tenant fuzzy search via pg_trgm word_similarity) breaks at runtime with 'function does not exist'. Installing into a dedicated extensions schema (not public) is the Supabase-recommended layout and keeps public clean, but then the schema must be on the search_path or every call must be schema-qualified; putting extensions AFTER public prevents an extension object from shadowing an application object (a known search_path-hijack footgun). Declaring only what is used (drop pgcrypto/uuid-ossp/unaccent) is least-surface. Ref: PostgreSQL CREATE EXTENSION / search_path; Supabase 'Manage extensions' + 'schema extensions' guidance.
changes.verification: As role api: unqualified `select word_similarity('cafe','café')` succeeds. `select extname from pg_extension order by 1` equals the expected set {plpgsql, vector, pg_trgm}. 99_verify block above raises 0 (nothing installed outside pg_catalog/extensions; pg_trgm present).

changes.what: Add a one-page prod transport-and-auth gate document for the Supabase-managed + self-hosted-VPS split, with a pass/fail checklist that ties each control to a 99_verify assertion or a documented managed-toggle check. grep of docs/migration/build-v3 for sslmode|pg_hba|scram|Enforce SSL|verify-full|IP allowlist returns nothing.
changes.where: New file docs/migration/build-v3/PROD_TRANSPORT_AUTH_GATE.md (the artifact set is DDL + 99_verify only today).
changes.sql_or_config:
# build-v3 prod transport/auth gate (each item = PASS/FAIL)
1. Supabase 'Enforce SSL on incoming connections' = ON            [managed toggle]
2. Both pools: sslmode=verify-full + pinned CA; pg_stat_ssl.ssl=t  [assert #6]
3. Supabase network restriction / IP allowlist -> VPS egress IP    [managed toggle]
4. Password auth = scram-sha-256 (md5 rejected)                    [select ... from pg_shadow / SHOW password_encryption]
5. DATABASE_URL_APP role: LOGIN, non-super, non-BYPASSRLS, member of api  [assert #3]
6. DATABASE_URL_WORKER role: BYPASSRLS by design (worker/postgres)  [documented]
7. Every tenant table: RLS ENABLED and FORCED                      [assert #4]
8. GUC name parity app.current_business (code == policy)           [assert #1]
9. log_statement='none' / no parameter logging for the api role    [managed / ALTER ROLE api SET log_statement='none']
10. Extensions = {plpgsql,vector,pg_trgm} in schema extensions only [assert #7]
11. No role but the owner holds UPDATE/DELETE on either audit_log   [assert #5]
changes.rationale: The split between Supabase-managed controls (Enforce SSL, network restriction, pg_hba/SCRAM) and self-managed controls (client sslmode, pool role, GUC name, extensions) is currently undefined, so managed toggles can ship at permissive defaults and the self-managed half is unowned. A single gate doc makes the instance-level security contract explicit and auditable, and converts findings #1/#3/#4/#6/#7 into a repeatable pre-cutover checklist. log_statement off for api prevents parameterized secrets (tokens, hashes) from being written to logs (CIS PostgreSQL logging guidance). Ref: CIS PostgreSQL Benchmark (TLS, authentication method scram-sha-256, logging); OWASP ASVS V1/V9 (verify security controls are defined and tested); Supabase production checklist.
changes.verification: Doc exists and every checklist row maps to either a passing 99_verify assertion (items 2,3,4,5,7,8,10,11) or a recorded managed-control check (items 1,3,9 — screenshot/CLI output). Cutover is blocked until all rows read PASS.

changes.what: Scrub Umi-internal Slack identifiers from the café-readable audit trail during backfill. Verified: all 20 migrated audit rows carry full before/after config blobs, and one embeds slack_channel_id='C0AJJEC73K7' / slack_channel_name='kalala-café-chapule' inside new_config, which lands in an RLS-scoped, café-readable tenant.audit_log.
changes.where: docs/migration/build-v3/backfill/backfill_growth.sql:123-135 (the tenant.audit_log INSERT ... SELECT).
changes.sql_or_config:
insert into tenant.audit_log
  (id, business_id, actor_user_id, action, entity, entity_id, before, after, at)
select
  al.id, al.tenant_id, null::uuid, 'update', 'business', al.tenant_id,
  (al.previous_config #- '{slack_channel_id}' #- '{slack_channel_name}') as before,
  (al.new_config      #- '{slack_channel_id}' #- '{slack_channel_name}') as after,
  al.changed_at

changes.rationale: tenant.audit_log is café-facing (a business owner reads their own change history under RLS); Umi-internal ops identifiers (Slack channel id/name) do not belong in a tenant-visible record and are exactly the kind of internal metadata data-minimization says to strip at import. The jsonb #- path operator removes the key without disturbing the rest of the config snapshot. A Slack channel id is an internal identifier, not a credential, so this is low-severity hygiene, but it is a one-line change at the point of import. Ref: OWASP data minimization / sensitive-data-exposure; GDPR-style minimization at ETL boundaries.
changes.verification: After backfill: `select count(*) from tenant.audit_log where before::text ~* 'slack_channel' or after::text ~* 'slack_channel'` = 0. Row count unchanged (still 20).

changes.what: Replace api's blanket SELECT on all of umi with catalog-only grants plus a SECURITY DEFINER credential-lookup function, and drop DELETE on the runtime auth tables — so one app-layer bug can no longer bulk-dump password_hash or hard-delete sessions/tokens. Verified: api holds SELECT on all 17 umi objects incl every column of umi.user (password_hash, password_algorithm) and full DML on runtime.otp/session/password_reset_token/device_session/pairing; the auth path even reads credentials on the BYPASSRLS worker/postgres pool (auth.repository.ts:49, vps-setup.md:147).
changes.where: docs/migration/build-v3/90_rls.sql:24,27 (replace the umi blanket SELECT + default-privilege line) and 26,29 (runtime verbs); new function in 10_umi.sql; backend auth read at apps/umi-api/src/modules/auth/auth.repository.ts:73-88 moves onto the api pool via the function.
changes.rationale: Least privilege + defense-in-depth: an IDOR/SQL-injection reachable through the request role should not be able to `SELECT password_hash FROM umi.user` for every tenant. Column-level GRANT removes the hash columns from api, and a SECURITY DEFINER function (owned by a privileged role, with a pinned search_path per PostgreSQL's SECURITY DEFINER guidance) turns a one-query bulk dump into a per-email oracle that still requires knowing each address — while keeping node-side scrypt verification intact. The catalogs the task mandates stay readable, and entitlement access is preserved through the non-security_invoker view (confirmed: effective_entitlement runs with owner privileges, so base-table SELECT on subscription/entitlement_override is unnecessary for api). Dropping DELETE on the auth tables prevents a bug from mass-revoking sessions (DoS) while INSERT/UPDATE (issue/consume/soft-revoke) remain. This only pays off once the auth path runs as api rather than the BYPASSRLS worker pool, so pair it with #1/#3. Ref: PostgreSQL GRANT (column privileges) + 'Writing SECURITY DEFINER Functions Safely'; OWASP A01 Broken Access Control / least privilege; CIS PostgreSQL least-privilege.
changes.verification: As role api: `select password_hash from umi.user limit 1` -> ERROR permission denied; `select * from umi.lookup_credential('x@y.z')` succeeds; `select count(*) from information_schema.role_column_grants where grantee='api' and table_schema='umi' and table_name='user' and column_name in ('password_hash','password_algorithm')` = 0; api retains SELECT on the 7 catalogs + effective_entitlement (count matches); api lost SELECT on subscription/subscription_item/invoice/entitlement_override/prospect/prospect_event/umi.audit_log; `select has_table_privilege('api','runtime.session','DELETE')` = false.

fixes.research_basis: PostgreSQL 17 manual: 'Row Security Policies' (policies not applied to table owner/superuser/BYPASSRLS; USING vs WITH CHECK), ALTER TABLE 'FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY', CREATE POLICY, System Administration Functions (current_setting/set_config), CREATE ROLE / role attributes (LOGIN, NOSUPERUSER, NOBYPASSRLS), GRANT (table + column-level privileges), 'Writing SECURITY DEFINER Functions Safely' (pin search_path), CREATE EXTENSION + schema/search_path, CREATE TRIGGER (fires for owner/superuser, unlike RLS), libpq SSL modes (verify-full authenticates the server; require/prefer do not). node-postgres (pg) documentation: TLS is off unless configured; ssl:{ca, rejectUnauthorized:true} required for verification. CIS PostgreSQL Benchmark: enforce TLS/SSL, authentication method scram-sha-256 (reject md5), restrict superuser/privileged roles, ensure audit records cannot be altered, logging configuration (avoid logging secrets). OWASP: Top 10 A01 Broken Access Control (least privilege, deny-by-default), Transport Layer Protection Cheat Sheet (TLS everywhere, cert validation), Authentication/Secrets storage, data minimization / A02 sensitive-data-exposure, ASVS V1/V9. Supabase docs: Row Level Security + FORCE, 'Manage database extensions' (dedicated extensions schema + search_path), 'Enforce SSL on incoming connections', network restrictions / IP allowlist, production readiness checklist, Supavisor pooler dotted-username role behavior. All findings re-confirmed empirically on umi_build_v3 / umi_backfill_v3 (PGPORT=5233): GUC drift (0 vs 20 rows), empty-string cast abort and nullif fail-closed, 33/33 tables ENABLED-not-FORCED, api blanket SELECT incl umi.user password columns + full DML on runtime auth tables, effective_entitlement is a non-security_invoker view, zero audit_log triggers, pg_trgm absent/unqualified-unresolvable due to empty role search_path, and prod auth reading credentials on the BYPASSRLS postgres worker pool.
fixes.risks: Ordering/coupling: changes #1, #3 and #10 are interdependent — if the backend GUC is renamed (#1) but the request pool still connects as postgres/umi_worker (BYPASSRLS), RLS stays off and #10's credential lockdown is moot; apply the role reconciliation (#3) and route the auth read onto the api pool in the same cutover step. #10 requires a backend change (auth.repository.ts must call umi.lookup_credential and select explicit umi.user columns, not SELECT *), so shipping the GRANT changes without the code change would break login with 'permission denied for column password_hash'; land them together and gate with an integration test. #4 FORCE RLS is safe for the app but WILL bite any admin/maintenance connection that relied on owner-bypass (e.g. ad hoc dashboards) — those must use the worker/BYPASSRLS role or SET app.current_business explicitly. #6 verify-full needs the correct Supabase CA PEM present on the VPS and readable at boot; a wrong/missing CA fails closed (no DB connection) — that is the intended behavior but must be provisioned before cutover, and CA rotation must be tracked. #7 dropping pgcrypto/uuid-ossp/unaccent assumes the grep-confirmed 'unused' status holds across all repos and future phases; re-run the usage grep at cutover and add back any that a later phase introduces (declare-what-you-use, not remove-blindly). Putting extensions after public on the search_path avoids object-shadowing but means any code that assumed an extension function shadows a same-named public object would change resolution — none found, but worth a grep. #5 append-only triggers make audit rows truly immutable, which is the goal, but any legitimate audit-correction workflow (e.g. GDPR erasure of a mis-logged PII value) must now go through the worker with the trigger temporarily disabled under change control, not an ad hoc UPDATE. #9 is data-only and low-risk but is a backfill-source edit; re-run the reconcile counts after. The umi.user_role SELECT grant in #10 still lets api read every café's role grants (umi has no RLS); this is metadata, not credentials/financials, and is called out as a follow-up to scope via a function keyed on app.current_business/app.user_id rather than fixed here. None of these changes touch the superuser-run backfill (it bypasses RLS and holds all privileges), and none weaken tenant RLS — every change is fail-closed or additive hardening.

fixes.summary: Six confirmed data-area defects share one root theme: the build-v3 backfill copies PII and dead provenance into tables that lack a tenant defense (runtime has 0/16 RLS; umi 0/16; only reminder_sent carries business_id), while every café request runs as `api`, which holds blanket SELECT on all umi (incl umi.user.password_hash) and full DML on all 16 runtime tables. I verified in umi_backfill_v3: 435/447 customers carry a DOB while 0/5 programs enable birthday rewards; 348 initial-balance ledger rows carry a dead umi-cash cuid in external_ref (mislabeled "Zettle uuid") that is fully redundant with idempotency_key; 417 outbox rows carry recipient phone + full Spanish message body + dropped turn_id/trace_id telemetry and 383 inbound rows carry WhatsApp profile_name — all into sealed, unscoped, cross-tenant-readable runtime tables; 432/458 contacts store the same phone in raw_phone_number and normalized_value; and umi.user holds 6 scrypt + 2 unsalted legacy-sha256 + 1 null archival hashes, all status='active'. The fix set is pure data-minimization + defense-in-depth: stop carrying data with zero product use, null dead pointers, add business_id + RLS so runtime machinery can be scoped (with the api-grant lockdown coordinated with the access/credentials area), harden the café-writable FK against the RLS-bypass oracle, and force-rotate the weak archival hashes. None of these break tenant RLS (backfill runs as superuser = bypasses RLS; worker is BYPASSRLS) and all keep the global umi catalogs readable to api.

changes.what: Stop carrying customer date-of-birth in the loyalty backfill (data minimization).
changes.where: docs/migration/build-v3/backfill/backfill_loyalty_v3.sql lines 14-17 (customer <- core.people insert).

-- 2. customer <- core.people  (birth_date NOT carried: birthday_reward_enabled=false
--    on every program, so DOB drives zero product function -> do not import sensitive PII)
insert into tenant.customer (id, business_id, name, birthday, loyalty_status, created_at, updated_at)

Re-collect only when a café actually turns birthday rewards on (which today carries 0 rows anyway if you gate instead of null).

changes.what: Do not migrate the historical outbox envelopes at all, and reduce inbound to a minimal dedup projection with an empty payload (webhook PII minimization).

-- 4) runtime.outbox_event  <-  NOT MIGRATED.
--    Delivered/failed delivery machinery: 417 rows, 394 carry recipient phone in
--    payload.to and the full message body in payload.body, plus dropped turn_id/
--    trace_id telemetry. The conversation history is already retained RLS-scoped in
--    tenant.message; the worker's read-back only targets status='pending' (0 pending).
--    => carry nothing. (delete the section-4 insert)

-- 5) runtime.inbound_event  <-  queue.inbound_events  (MINIMAL dedup projection only)
insert into runtime.inbound_event (id, provider, external_id, payload, status,
                                   received_at, processed_at, created_at)

       'processed', i.received_at, i.completed_at, i.received_at
from queue.inbound_events i
where i.received_at > now() - interval '3 days';
changes.rationale: runtime.inbound_event/outbox_event have no business_id and no RLS, yet api holds full DML and readonly holds SELECT, so these are cross-tenant readable. The envelopes duplicate PII already retained in RLS-scoped tenant.message + tenant.contact into a weaker (sealed, unscoped) location — net effect is strictly weaker protection with zero forward value (payload jsonb is NOT NULL, so we carry '{}' for the dedup unique index). This is GDPR Art 5(1)(c) minimization + defense-in-depth: never store the raw envelope; store a minimal typed projection. Outbox forward-value is nil because the pending-index read-back path only targets status='pending' (0 rows) and only 2 failed rows exist.

changes.what: Document that raw_phone_number and normalized_value are ONE PII field stored twice, so any masking/erasure routine and the lookup index are covered.

The phone itself is product-needed (loyalty soft-key), so it stays — but a future GDPR erasure/tokenization that nulls only one column silently leaves the other populated and the normalized copy is also indexed (contact_lookup_idx). Documenting the one-field-two-columns fact is the low-cost guard (OWASP data-handling / GDPR Art 17 right-to-erasure completeness).
changes.verification: Erasure-routine test: after redacting a contact's phone, `select count(*) from tenant.contact where id=:erased and (raw_phone_number is not null or normalized_value is not null);` must return 0. Also assert both comments exist on tenant.contact.

changes.what: Add business_id to the webhook/queue runtime tables and enable RLS on the tenant-attributable runtime tables (defense-in-depth); reduce api's blanket runtime DML to worker-only for the sealed machinery (coordinated with the access/credentials area).
changes.where: docs/migration/build-v3/30_runtime.sql (inbound_event ~line 97, outbox_event ~line 83, dead_letter ~line 118); new RLS block + grant revoke in docs/migration/build-v3/90_rls.sql (after line 29).
changes.rationale: Verified 0/16 runtime tables have RLS and api holds SELECT+INSERT+UPDATE+DELETE on all 16, including inbound_event whose 395 cross-tenant rows I read as api scoped to one business. 15/16 tables lack business_id so they are structurally impossible to scope until the column exists (PostgreSQL RLS §5.9). The data-owned pieces here are (a) the business_id columns and (b) RLS on the tables that CAN be scoped. The queue/webhook/auth machinery that legitimately spans tenants should not be api-reachable at all — least privilege (CIS PostgreSQL Benchmark: revoke excessive privileges; keep grants minimal) — so it becomes worker-only, and api reaches the few auth flows it needs via SECURITY DEFINER functions (credentials-area work). Backfill is unaffected (superuser bypasses RLS) and worker is unaffected (BYPASSRLS).
changes.verification: `select count(*) from information_schema.role_table_grants where grantee='api' and table_schema='runtime' and table_name in ('otp','password_reset_token','session','inbound_event','outbox_event','dead_letter','idempotency_key');` = 0. And `select count(*) from pg_class c join pg_namespace n on n.oid=c.relnamespace where n.nspname='runtime' and c.relkind='r' and not c.relrowsecurity and c.relname in ('reminder_sent','integration_sync','conversation_state');` = 0. (Today: api grant count = 7+; RLS-missing count > 0.)

changes.what: Close the FK-bypass existence oracle / cross-tenant reference contamination on café-writable FKs using composite (business_id, id) references, plus app-layer target validation and FK-error suppression.
changes.where: docs/migration/build-v3/20_tenant.sql (tenant.customer add unique; tenant.customer_order.customer_id FK at line ~399) and the umi-api write path.
changes.sql_or_config:
-- allow tenant-scoped composite references
alter table tenant.customer add constraint customer_business_id_uq unique (business_id, id);

-- re-point the café-writable FK so a foreign-tenant customer FAILS the constraint
alter table tenant.customer_order drop constraint customer_order_customer_id_fkey;
alter table tenant.customer_order
  add constraint customer_order_customer_fk
  foreign key (business_id, customer_id)
  references tenant.customer (business_id, id) on delete cascade;

-- App layer (umi-api): before any write, validate user-supplied FK UUIDs resolve
-- within current_setting('app.current_business'); catch 23503 and return a generic
-- 'invalid reference' — never echo the Postgres FK constraint detail to the client.
changes.rationale: PostgreSQL RLS §5.9 explicitly bypasses row security for referential-integrity checks, so a scoped api can attach its order to a foreign-tenant customer (accept) vs a bogus uuid (FK error) — an accept/error existence oracle plus dangling cross-tenant references (reproduced in umi_backfill_v3). Kalala has 51 orders, so the writable surface is real. A composite (business_id, customer_id) FK forces the referenced row to share the acting business, so a foreign target fails the constraint outright — the bypass no longer leaks membership. App-layer validation + generic error text (OWASP ASVS V7 / CWE-209 improper error handling) removes the residual timing/error-text oracle. Impact is low (SELECT-back stays blocked by base-table RLS), so scope composite FKs to café-writable sibling FKs like customer_order.customer_id.
changes.verification: As api scoped to business A: `begin; set local role api; set local app.current_business='<A>'; update tenant.customer_order set customer_id='<a business-B customer>' where id='<an A order>';` must raise FK violation (0 rows), whereas the same update to an A-owned customer succeeds. (Today: the foreign update returns UPDATE 1.)

changes.what: Data-clean the archival password-hash corpus: force-rotate the weak legacy-sha256 accounts (and prefer a cutover-wide reset given N=9); this pairs with the credentials-area column lockdown.
changes.where: umi.user data (backfill / cutover policy step); column-privilege lockdown on umi.user.password_hash/password_algorithm is the credentials-area lever, referenced here.
changes.sql_or_config:
-- Force-rotate the unacceptable unsalted legacy hashes (2 rows). Backend treats
-- password_hash IS NULL + status='active' as 'must set a password at next login'.
update umi.user set password_hash = null, password_algorithm = null
 where password_algorithm = 'legacy-sha256-v1';

-- PREFERRED given only 9 operators total: eliminate the entire archival hash store
-- with a cutover-wide reset (uncomment if ops accepts a one-time reset email):
-- update umi.user set password_hash = null, password_algorithm = null;
changes.rationale: Verified umi.user = 6 scrypt-sha256-v1 (modern, salted+stretched — acceptable) + 2 legacy-sha256-v1 (unsalted SHA-256 — an unacceptable password store per OWASP Password Storage Cheat Sheet) + 1 null, all 9 status='active'. Reasoning the archival decision explicitly: (1) do NOT blanket-null hashes for active users without a reset flow or you lock out every operator; (2) the scrypt hashes can be carried as-is and rehashed lazily on next successful login; (3) the 2 legacy hashes MUST be force-rotated because they are cryptographically weak; (4) because the whole corpus is only 9 users, the cleanest posture is a cutover password reset that removes ALL archival hashes from the new store — operationally trivial and it also shrinks the blast radius of the separate finding that api currently holds blanket SELECT on umi.user.password_hash. The column-level revoke (api must not SELECT password_hash/password_algorithm; verification via SECURITY DEFINER) is owned by the credentials/access area and is the complementary control.
changes.verification: `select count(*) from umi.user where password_algorithm='legacy-sha256-v1';` = 0 after the clean. If the preferred cutover reset is taken: `select count(*) from umi.user where password_hash is not null;` = 0. (Today: 2 legacy, 8 with hash.)

fixes.research_basis: PostgreSQL 16 docs §5.9 Row Security Policies — RLS is enforced per-command but "referential integrity checks ... foreign key references, always bypass row security," which is the exact mechanism behind the FK existence-oracle finding, and the same section documents ALTER TABLE ... ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + BYPASSRLS (worker) that make the runtime-scoping fix safe for backfill (superuser) and worker. PostgreSQL GRANT/§5.8 privileges + CIS PostgreSQL Benchmark (Section 4/6: revoke excessive table privileges, revoke PUBLIC, grant least privilege to application roles) ground reducing api's blanket runtime DML to worker-only sealed machinery. OWASP Privacy-by-Design / Data Minimization and GDPR Art 5(1)(c) (store only data with a defined purpose) ground dropping the 435 unused DOBs and the raw webhook envelopes; GDPR Art 17 (right to erasure completeness) grounds the dual-column phone documentation. OWASP Password Storage Cheat Sheet (unsalted SHA-256 is not an acceptable store; migrate/force-rotate weak hashes, rehash-on-login for acceptable ones) grounds the archival-hash decision. OWASP ASVS V7 / CWE-209 (improper error handling — do not leak internal constraint detail) grounds suppressing FK-constraint error text at the app layer.
fixes.risks: All changes are additive/subtractive to a pre-cutover from-scratch build and run as superuser, so none break tenant RLS or the backfill, and worker (BYPASSRLS) is unaffected. Specific risks: (1) Dropping birthday means any café that later enables birthday rewards must re-collect DOBs — acceptable, and consistent with collecting at point of use. (2) Nulling external_ref loses nothing because the cuid provenance survives in idempotency_key; the only risk is future code that (wrongly) reads external_ref for migration provenance — mitigated by the corrected comment. (3) Not migrating outbox/inbound drops delivery-status history and raw envelopes; these are working-memory machinery (nothing reads delivered rows back), and the conversation content persists RLS-scoped in tenant.message, so forward value is nil — the only edge is losing a >3-day webhook-redelivery dedup window, which at a from-scratch cutover is moot. (4) Adding business_id + RLS to runtime and revoking api's runtime grants requires the credentials-area SECURITY DEFINER auth functions to land together, or the login/OTP/session flows api needs would lose access — this change MUST be sequenced with the access/credentials-area fix, not shipped alone. (5) The composite-FK change requires business_id to be immutable on tenant.customer/customer_order (it is, in practice) and adds one unique index per referenced parent; it only re-points café-writable sibling FKs, so it does not disturb umi<->tenant cross-schema FKs. (6) Force-rotating the 2 legacy hashes (or the full cutover reset) requires a working password-reset/first-login flow to exist at cutover, else those operators are locked out — verify the reset path before applying. None of these should be applied now; they are proposals for the orchestrator to sequence.

gaps: POOLER / SET-LOCAL ISOLATION not gated. Supabase fronts connections with Supavisor (transaction pooling). The RLS linchpin only holds if app.current_business is set via set_config(...,true) INSIDE the same transaction as the query. No proposal gates against (a) a plain session-level SET leaking the GUC to the next tenant's transaction on a shared backend, (b) an autocommit path where set_config(...,true) and the SELECT run as two separate transactions so the GUC is lost, or (c) the confirmed pre-existing leak that the DEFAULT PgService.query() path runs on the BYPASSRLS worker pool with manual WHERE predicates (F5 notes it; no fix/gate closes it). This is a live multi-tenant-isolation hole distinct from the GUC-name drift.

SCRAM / login-role password type is asserted only for the DB-wide setting, never for the actual login roles. password_encryption=scram-sha-256 is confirmed, but api/worker/readonly are NOLOGIN (no password); the real request identities are the new *_login roles. No gate asserts pg_shadow.passwdtype is SCRAM-SHA-256 (not md5) for those login roles, nor that Supabase rejects md5. pg_hba.conf itself is Supabase-managed/immutable, so the only 'ours' control is verifying every login role's stored verifier is SCRAM — that check is missing.

FDW server / user-mapping / dblink credential remnants are ungated. Cutover carries DATA via FDW replay and the source is umi_prod_snapshot; the injection finding only gates that 2 legacy SECURITY DEFINER functions are excluded. A leftover postgres_fdw SERVER + USER MAPPING (or dblink) in the shipped target embeds the source DB password and is a standing cross-DB read path — no proposal asserts the target has zero foreign servers/user mappings.

public-schema baseline hardening not asserted. No gate does REVOKE CREATE ON SCHEMA public FROM PUBLIC or asserts PUBLIC lacks CREATE on any schema — the CVE-2018-1058 enabler that makes the (correctly-noted) unpinned trigger search_paths matter. The trigger search_path pins are proposed but the complementary 'no writable schema on the search path' invariant is not gated.

Log/parameter redaction for the api role is mentioned once in a gate checklist but has no fix + verification. Token/OTP/reset hashes and business ids flow as bound parameters; without log_statement='none' and no parameter logging for the request role, an enabled statement/parameter log becomes a credential sink that survives all the grant lockdowns.

No gate forbids tenant-scoped request reads from EVER executing on a BYPASSRLS connection (worker/service_role). The boot guard only inspects the app pool's own role; nothing asserts that repository code for tenant data uses the RLS pool rather than workerTx()/query(), so the isolation can be silently bypassed one repository call at a time.

Residual outbound-message-injection is not fully closed and not called out as accepted risk. Some proposals keep api INSERT on runtime.outbox_event (append-only); that still lets a request-path bug enqueue an arbitrary outbound WhatsApp/Slack message as any café (the core of F1's blast radius beyond auth). Either route enqueue through a definer that stamps origin business_id, or explicitly accept and document the residual.
over_engineering: The optional 'deeper layer' that adds umi.session_can_access_business(business_id) as an EXISTS subquery to all 40+ tenant/runtime policies (rls-area change 6). For 5 cafés pre-cutover the single SECURITY DEFINER choke-point (set_business_context validating the grant before setting the GUC) is sufficient; per-policy EXISTS multiplies planner cost on every row of every query for marginal defense-in-depth. Keep the choke-point + wired role_permission; skip the per-policy binding.

Composite (business_id, id) FKs applied across 'every tenant FK to a sibling tenant table' (F8 / data-area change 6). The oracle is confirmed LOW severity (SELECT-back stays RLS-blocked; grafted money lands under the attacker's own business_id). A schema-wide composite-FK refactor plus ON DELETE CASCADE rewrites is disproportionate; app-layer target validation + generic FK error text covers it. If any composite FK is worth it, limit it to the money-bearing writable ones (loyalty_card/customer_order → customer), not the whole graph.

Reach-through RLS with EXISTS subqueries on the three runtime embedding tables (product/message/knowledge_embedding). These are sealed RAG artifacts; the simpler control already proposed elsewhere — revoke readonly SELECT and keep them off the request path / behind the worker — achieves the isolation goal without a per-row EXISTS join into tenant.product/message on every similarity read. Reserve reach-through RLS for the runtime tables api legitimately touches on the request path (conversation_state, pass_device, reminder_sent).

The full dedicated auth role (authsvc/auth_owner) + separate connection pool + per-request SECURITY DEFINER session-validation RPC on the hot path (privileges change 1 / credentials change 2-3). Given the salt is dropped so login must be reset anyway, the 80/20 for pre-cutover is: revoke api's DML on the runtime auth tables and move session/OTP/reset ISSUANCE onto the existing worker. A brand-new principal, a third pool, and a definer RPC on every authenticated request is a larger build than a 5-café pre-cutover cutover needs; defensible but sequence it after the revokes, not as a blocker.

Three-to-four overlapping proposals (rls, privileges, credentials, config areas) each rewrite the SAME 90_rls.sql grant block and re-add SECURITY DEFINER credential functions with slightly different names/shapes. Consolidate to ONE grant-rewrite + ONE auth-function file; shipping all of them as-authored would produce conflicting REVOKE/GRANT ordering and duplicate functions.
corrections: effective_entitlement contradiction = false security. Verified: the view body is a UNION ALL over umi.subscription with NO business_id predicate. Therefore (a) the credentials/config-area proposals that KEEP it non-security_invoker specifically 'so api resolves entitlement without base grants' preserve the confirmed cross-tenant leak (6 rows / 2 businesses) — a working view that still leaks; and (b) merely flipping it to security_invoker=true (rls-area) does NOT fix it either, because umi base tables have 0 RLS, so it still returns all tenants. The ONLY correct fixes are: security_invoker=true AND enable RLS on umi.subscription/entitlement_override(/plan_feature) scoped to app.current_business AND keep api's SELECT on those base tables (api currently HAS it — verified t); OR keep owner-rights but add `WHERE s.business_id = nullif(current_setting('app.current_business',true),'')::uuid` inside BOTH UNION arms. Pick one coherent path; as written the proposals cancel out.

'Carry the 6 scrypt hashes and rehash lazily on next successful login' (data-area change 7; echoed in config change 10) is technically impossible and gives false confidence. Every finding establishes the per-user salt was dropped and umi.user has no salt column, so scrypt(password, salt) can never be recomputed — those hashes can NEVER verify a login, so the rehash-on-login trigger never fires. Carrying them is dead, opaque, credential-shaped weight. The correct, internally-consistent action (which the same proposals also state) is NULL password_hash+password_algorithm for all 9 users and force status='invited'/reset at cutover. Delete the 'keep + rehash' sub-recommendation.

api_login `NOINHERIT` + `GRANT api TO api_login` (config change 3) breaks all app access. With NOINHERIT the login role receives none of api's privileges unless it issues `SET ROLE api` on every connection; as written every request gets permission-denied. Use INHERIT (or simply give the api role LOGIN + a SCRAM password). RLS keys on the app.current_business GUC, not on the role name, so an INHERITing api_login is fully RLS-enforced — there is no isolation reason to prefer NOINHERIT here.

Do not sell FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY as closing the owner/BYPASSRLS bypass. Verified locally: the Supabase object-owner role postgres has rolbypassrls=f (so FORCE DOES contain an owner-role connection — good), but service_role and worker have rolbypassrls=t and FORCE never applies to a BYPASSRLS role. So FORCE addresses only the plain-owner connection; the actual isolation-defeating path (request routed through service_role/worker/BYPASSRLS) is UNAFFECTED by FORCE and must be closed by role reconciliation + the boot guard, not by FORCE. Keep FORCE (cheap, real for the owner case) but don't let it create confidence that the BYPASSRLS bypass is handled.

Conflicting search_path role settings across proposals: one sets `search_path = tenant, umi, runtime, extensions, public`, another `search_path = "$user", public, extensions`. Reconcile to ONE. extensions must come AFTER public (an extension object must not be able to shadow an application object); and there is no need to put tenant/umi/runtime on the path since the DDL and app use schema-qualified names — doing so only widens the name-resolution/shadowing surface. Recommended: `"$user", public, extensions`.

Keeping `alter default privileges in schema umi, tenant grant select on tables to readonly` (config change 6) contradicts the minimization goal the same doc argues: a future umi credential/token table would be auto-exposed to the reporting role the instant it is created. Either drop readonly defaults entirely and grant per-table, or scope them to schemas that never hold secrets (not umi/runtime).

Composite-FK change (data-area 6 / rls-area 7) must preserve original delete semantics and NOT-NULL invariants. It rewrites customer_order→customer to ON DELETE CASCADE and requires business_id to be NOT NULL and immutable on every child. Verify business_id is NOT NULL on all rewritten children first (or the FK/insert fails), and keep the pre-existing ON DELETE action (introducing CASCADE silently deletes orders when a customer is removed — a behavior change, not a security fix).

Inconsistent api-on-runtime grants: one proposal grants api INSERT on runtime.outbox_event/inbound_event (append), another revokes ALL runtime from api and routes everything through worker/definer. Decide explicitly. If api retains INSERT on outbox_event, the F1 outbound-message-injection blast radius is only partly closed (api can still enqueue an arbitrary outbound message as any café); if you revoke it, ensure the legitimate request-path enqueue moves to a definer/worker or the app breaks. Do not ship both.

gate_sections.pass_criterion: On the app and readonly pools, `SELECT rolsuper, rolbypassrls FROM pg_roles WHERE rolname=current_user` returns (f,f) AND `pg_has_role(current_user,'api','member')` is true; the app boot guard aborts the process otherwise; DATABASE_URL_APP/DATABASE_URL_READONLY resolve to a role that is neither postgres nor service_role; the worker pool is the only pool whose current_user has rolbypassrls=t.

`SELECT count(*) FROM pg_class c JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid=c.relnamespace WHERE n.nspname='tenant' AND c.relkind='r' AND NOT (c.relrowsecurity AND c.relforcerowsecurity)` = 0, and every tenant base table has exactly one tenant_isolation policy (tables with policy count <>1 return empty).

0 tenant policies contain 'app.tenant_id' and 0 lack 'app.current_business'; the literal set_config key in pg.service.ts equals the policy GUC name (CI grep); as the login role: GUC unset → 0 rows and NO error, GUC='' → 0 rows and NO error (nullif guard present), GUC=business A → only A's rows; GUC set/query run in one transaction (integration test: tenant A context cannot read tenant B through the real app pool).

Every view in umi and tenant either has security_invoker=true OR contains an app.current_business predicate in its body; behavioral: api scoped to business A returns count == the RLS-scoped base-table count (NOT all tenants) from BOTH tenant.conversation_analytics AND umi.effective_entitlement; api holds no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on any view.

has_column_privilege for password_hash and password_algorithm on umi.user is false for both api and readonly; has_table_privilege on runtime.session/otp/password_reset_token/device_session/pairing (any of SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) is false for api and readonly; api retains SELECT on the global catalogs (umi.role/permission/feature/plan/plan_feature/channel_type) and EXECUTE on the defined auth SECURITY DEFINER functions.

api scoped to a business with zero conversations returns 0 rows from runtime.conversation_state and 0 foreign rows from runtime.pass_device/reminder_sent/integration_sync; api has zero DML on the runtime auth/queue machinery it does not serve; every runtime table api can read on the request path is either RLS-scoped to app.current_business or reachable only via a definer function.

`SELECT count(*) FROM pg_default_acl d JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid=d.defaclnamespace WHERE n.nspname IN ('umi','tenant','runtime') AND array_to_string(d.defaclacl,',') ~ '(^|,)(api|worker)='` = 0; any remaining default privilege targets only readonly on non-secret schemas.

Both pools set ssl:{ca,rejectUnauthorized:true} (or sslmode=verify-full&sslrootcert=...); at boot `SELECT ssl FROM pg_stat_ssl WHERE pid=pg_backend_pid()` returns t on every pool; a deliberately wrong CA causes connection failure (proves verify, not prefer); Supabase 'Enforce SSL on incoming connections' is ON.

`SHOW password_encryption` = 'scram-sha-256'; every LOGIN role used by the app has a SCRAM-SHA-256 verifier in pg_shadow (0 rows where passwd not like 'SCRAM-SHA-256%' among app login roles); no md5 verifier exists for any app role.

Installed extension set equals {plpgsql, vector, pg_trgm}, none installed outside pg_catalog/extensions; api/worker have USAGE (not CREATE) on schema extensions; as api, unqualified `'[1,2,3]'::vector` and `word_similarity('a','b')` both resolve.

Every SECURITY DEFINER function in umi/tenant/runtime is on an allowlist, has a pinned search_path, and has EXECUTE revoked from PUBLIC; the two shared trigger functions are SECURITY INVOKER with a pinned search_path; umi.audit_log and tenant.audit_log carry an append-only trigger and no non-owner role holds UPDATE/DELETE on either; no writable schema (incl. public via PUBLIC CREATE) is on any app role's search_path.

0 umi.user rows are status='active' with password_hash IS NULL; 0 rows retain a legacy-sha256 hash (target: all password_hash/password_algorithm NULL + status='invited'); the @umi.invalid ghost account is status='suspended'; no dead migration external_ref, no carried DOB where birthday_reward_enabled=false, no raw webhook payloads in runtime.inbound/outbox, and no Slack internal ids in tenant.audit_log.

Recorded evidence that the Supabase JWT secret and Twilio auth token were rotated after the leak and gitleaks/trufflehog over full history returns 0 live secrets; Supabase network restriction limits connections to the VPS egress IP; the target DB has zero foreign-data-wrapper servers/user mappings and no dblink credentials from the snapshot template; no application code path connects as service_role; log_statement='none' (and no parameter logging) for the request role.

critique.verdict: DIRECTION APPROVED, NOT YET SHIP-READY. The findings are empirically solid and the remediation spine is right: security_invoker/FORCE on tenant, strip api/readonly off the credential + auth-table surface, revoke blanket runtime DML, wire the empty RBAC map behind one SECURITY DEFINER choke-point, pin verify-full TLS, and gate it all in 99_verify. But the fix SET as authored is internally contradictory and would give false security in three concrete ways that must be resolved before cutover: (1) the effective_entitlement proposals cancel out — verified the view has no business filter, so 'keep owner-rights to avoid base grants' preserves the confirmed cross-tenant leak and 'just add security_invoker' also leaks while umi has 0 RLS; pick security_invoker + RLS-on-umi-base + keep api base SELECT, or add an in-view app.current_business predicate. (2) 'Carry scrypt hashes and rehash on login' is impossible because the salt was dropped — NULL all 9 + invite. (3) api_login NOINHERIT breaks all app access, and FORCE RLS must not be sold as closing the BYPASSRLS bypass (service_role/worker still bypass; postgres-owner does not — verified). Then close the genuinely-missed gates: pooler/SET-LOCAL isolation + the pre-existing worker-pool default read path, SCRAM verifier on the actual login roles, FDW/dblink credential remnants, public-schema CREATE, and api-log redaction. Trim the over-scoped pieces (per-policy app.current_user EXISTS, schema-wide composite FKs, embedding-table reach-through RLS, and the third auth pool) so the pre-cutover pass stays proportionate to a 5-café system, and consolidate the four overlapping 90_rls.sql rewrites into one. Ship the gate document with the sections above, each blocking cutover on its objective check.
